name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday loans ($100–$500), installment loans ($100–$1,000), and title loans ($2,000–$25,000) with same-day funding at physical locations and online.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized lender specializing in short-term emergency cash solutions. The company operates physical store locations across the U.S., including the Jacksonville, FL branch at 1020-14 N. Edgewood Ave., where customers can apply in person or online. The company has been serving customers seeking fast access to funds between paychecks for years and maintains a 4.9-star rating across 125,585+ reviews on Google.

Advance America offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans (2–4 week terms, $100–$500), Installment Loans (multiple payments over time, $100–$1,000), and Title Loans (collateral-based using a vehicle, $2,000–$25,000). All products are available online or in-store. The Jacksonville location operates Monday–Thursday 10 a.m.–6 p.m., Friday 9 a.m.–6 p.m., and Saturday 9 a.m.–4 p.m. The company also offers Western Union services and provides bilingual support ("Hablamos español").

The company distinguishes itself through convenience—same-day funding, multiple loan types to match different needs, and a hybrid application model (online or in-store). The high customer review rating and emphasis on trained staff explains suggests strong operational execution. Title loans in particular offer significantly higher amounts ($25,000) compared to payday products, appealing to customers with vehicle collateral.

However, Advance America operates in the high-cost lending sector. The website does not disclose APR, fees, or total cost of borrowing—critical information absent from marketing materials. Payday and installment loans typically carry triple-digit APRs. These products are designed as short-term solutions but can trap borrowers in debt cycles. The absence of transparent pricing on the website is a significant red flag for consumer protection.
pros: Same-day funding available for payday and installment loans up to $1,000
Higher loan amounts for title loans ($2,000–$25,000) compared to payday-only lenders
Multiple loan products (payday, installment, title) to match different borrower needs
4.9-star rating across 125,585+ Google reviews indicating consistent customer satisfaction
Bilingual support (Spanish) and trained staff to explain loan options
Flexible application: online, in-store, or phone pre-qualification
Extended store hours (open Saturday mornings; weekday hours until 6 p.m.)
cons: APR, fees, and total cost of borrowing not disclosed on website—no transparency on actual cost
Payday and installment loans are high-cost products typically carrying triple-digit APRs
Short repayment terms (2–4 weeks for payday loans) increase default risk and debt cycle potential
Title loans require vehicle collateral, creating risk of vehicle loss if borrower cannot repay
No mention of credit counseling, debt management, or alternatives to high-cost borrowing
best_for: Borrowers with immediate cash needs ($100–$1,000) and steady income for repayment within weeks
Vehicle owners needing larger emergency funds ($2,000–$25,000) and willing to use car as collateral
Consumers who prefer in-person interaction and explanation from trained loan officers before committing
diagnosis: Advance America is best for consumers facing genuine short-term cash emergencies who have income to repay within weeks and understand the high cost of borrowing. The critical caveat is that the company does not disclose APR or fees on its website; borrowers must contact the store or apply to learn actual costs, and typical rates far exceed alternatives like credit union PALs (capped at 36% APR) or employer advances.
services: Installment Loans ($100–$1,000, multiple payments over time)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
